name: linear-projects-read
description: List and get Linear projects via CLI (read-only operations)
capabilities: List all Linear projects with filtering options
Filter projects by status (planned, started, paused, completed, canceled)
Filter projects by project lead name
Retrieve specific project details by name or ID
Output results as JSON or formatted table
author: Bind
securitySummary: This is a read-only Linear integration skill. The static analyzer incorrectly flagged documentation and metadata as security vulnerabilities. All 47 detected patterns are false positives: documentation URLs (not network exfil), standard .env configuration patterns (not sensitive exposure), bash command examples (not shell execution), and text strings misidentified as weak cryptography. The skill makes authenticated read-only API calls to Linear's official servers with no malicious behavior.
valueStatement: Managing multiple Linear projects requires context switching and manual searching. This skill provides instant access to list and retrieve project information directly from your CLI. Access project details, filter by status or lead, and retrieve specific projects without leaving your workflow.
limitations: Requires LINEAR_API_KEY environment variable or .env file
Read-only operations - cannot create, update, or delete projects
Partial name matching is case-insensitive only

useCases.title: Review active projects
useCases.description: Quickly review all started projects with their leads to track team progress.

useCases.title: Find project details
useCases.description: Look up project information by name to understand context before starting work.

useCases.title: Audit project status
useCases.description: Generate filtered project lists to prepare for sprint planning meetings.

promptTemplates.title: List recent projects
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to see all projects
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the list-projects command to show all Linear projects.

promptTemplates.title: Filter by status
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need active projects only
promptTemplates.prompt: List all projects with status started using the list-projects command.

promptTemplates.title: Find project by name
promptTemplates.scenario: When you know the project name
promptTemplates.prompt: Get details for the project named Mount Vernon using the get-project command.

promptTemplates.title: Export as JSON
promptTemplates.prompt: List the 10 most recent started projects and output the results as JSON.

bestPractices: Store your LINEAR_API_KEY securely in environment variables rather than in code
Use the --json flag when you need to process project data programmatically
Combine filters like --status and --lead to narrow down results quickly
antiPatterns: Do not re-summarize or reformat table output that is already visible to the user
Do not attempt to create or modify projects with this read-only skill
Do not share your LINEAR_API_KEY in logs, error messages, or with others

faq.question: Which AI tools work with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Each tool can invoke the list-projects and get-project commands.

faq.question: What are the rate limits for Linear API?
faq.answer: Linear API has standard rate limits. The skill defaults to 25 results to avoid hitting limits. Use --limit to adjust.

faq.question: How do I set up the Linear API key?
faq.answer: Generate a Personal API key from Linear Settings > API. Set it as LINEAR_API_KEY in your environment or .env file.

faq.question: Is my project data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only reads data from Linear using your authenticated API key. No data is written or modified.

faq.question: Why am I seeing authentication errors?
faq.answer: Check that LINEAR_API_KEY is set correctly in your environment. Verify the key has read permissions in Linear settings.

faq.question: How does this compare to Linear web interface?
faq.answer: This skill provides CLI access for faster workflows. The web interface offers more advanced filtering and editing capabilities.
